senses.definition: situated next to or near something

examples.x: The bookstore is located in the shopping mall adjacent to the coffee shop.
Our hotel room had a beautiful view of the park adjacent to the river.
The new playground was built adjacent to the elementary school.
Please park your car in the spaces adjacent to the main entrance.

senses.definition: relating to the study or art of constructing or designing a building

examples.x: The city skyline is a testament to its rich architectural heritage, featuring a blend of modern skyscrapers and historic landmarks.
The architectural firm won an award for its innovative design of the new museum building.
Students in the architectural program study a wide range of topics, from structural engineering to architectural history.

senses.definition: located on the inside part of a particular thing

examples.x: The interior cabin of the spacecraft was equipped with state-of-the-art technology for the astronauts' comfort and safety.
The interior lining of the jacket provides extra warmth in cold weather.
They inspected the interior compartments of the suitcase before packing.

senses.definition: located on the outer surface of a particular thing

examples.x: The building’s exterior surfaces are designed to withstand extreme weather.
Exterior lights were installed along the pathway for better visibility.
The car’s exterior paint had faded after years in the sun.

senses.definition: covered with a substance that does not let heat, electricity, or sound to enter or escape through it

examples.x: The insulated walls of the house helped maintain a comfortable indoor temperature year-round.
The insulated windows prevented heat from escaping during the winter months, reducing energy costs.
The insulated electrical wiring in the building ensured safety and minimized the risk of electrical fires.
The insulated attic space kept the interior of the house cool in the summer and warm in the winter.
The insulated soundproofing panels in the recording studio minimized outside noise, allowing for high-quality audio recordings.

senses.definition: involving or belonging to the government of a city, town, etc.

examples.x: Municipal parks provide green spaces for residents to enjoy outdoor recreational activities.
The municipal library offers a wide range of resources and services to the local community.
Municipal regulations require homeowners to obtain permits for certain construction projects.
Municipal utilities ensure reliable access to essential services such as water and electricity for residents.

senses.definition: (of an area with buildings) designed specially for people to live in

examples.x: The residential neighborhood is characterized by tree-lined streets and single-family homes.
Residential leases often include clauses outlining tenant responsibilities for property maintenance.
Residential streets are typically quieter and less congested than major thoroughfares.

senses.definition: (of a place) remote and without any appealing or interesting qualities

examples.x: After hours of driving through the godforsaken desert, they finally stumbled upon a small oasis.
The godforsaken village had been abandoned for decades, with only ruins remaining as a testament to its former existence.
Lost in the godforsaken wilderness, they began to fear they would never find their way back to civilization.
The godforsaken battlefield bore witness to the horrors of war, with shattered ruins and unmarked graves.

senses.definition: characteristic of or relating to a residential area outside a city or town

examples.x: The suburban neighborhood was known for its quiet streets and spacious homes.
Many families prefer suburban living for its sense of community and access to amenities.
The suburban commute to the city took longer than expected due to heavy traffic.
Suburban schools are known for their high-quality education programs and extracurricular activities.

senses.definition: (of a house, room, seat, etc.) empty or unoccupied and available to be used

examples.x: The vacant house at the end of the street had been abandoned for years.
She found a vacant seat near the window and settled in for the long train journey.
The hotel had a few vacant rooms available for last-minute bookings.
There is a vacant apartment available for rent in the building.
He found a vacant spot on the beach to lay his towel.
The vacant parking spaces quickly filled up when the event started.

senses.definition: to make something by putting separate parts of something together

examples.x: The team of workers assembled the furniture according to the provided instructions.
Assembling the model airplane required carefully piecing together the various components.
The car manufacturer assembles vehicles by combining different parts on the assembly line.
Students were given kits to assemble simple robots as part of a science project.

senses.definition: (of a structure or an area) to become worse gradually

examples.x: The old house began to decay after years of neglect.
The abandoned buildings slowly decayed over time.
Without maintenance, the bridge will decay and collapse.
The town started to decay as people moved away.
Rain and wind caused the wooden fence to decay.

senses.definition: to completely destroy or to knock down a building or another structure

examples.x: The old factory was demolished to make way for a new development.
The wrecking ball was used to demolish the abandoned building.
The earthquake had the power to demolish poorly constructed houses.
The city decided to demolish the unsafe bridge for public safety.
The construction crew will demolish the existing walls before rebuilding.

senses.definition: to build or assemble a structure or object in an upright position

examples.x: The construction crew worked diligently to erect a new office building in the city center.
Engineers were tasked with erecting a sturdy bridge over the river to improve transportation.
To mark the historical site, the city decided to erect a monument in honor of the founding fathers.
The team successfully erected a large tent for the outdoor event, providing shelter for attendees.
The company planned to erect a solar power plant to harness clean energy for the community.

senses.definition: to make a room or building look more attractive by repairing, redecorating, or cleaning it

examples.x: They refurbished the old house to make it livable again.
The hotel was refurbished with modern furniture and decor.
He refurbished the antique table to restore its original beauty.
The company refurbishes used laptops before selling them.
The museum was refurbished to attract more visitors.

senses.definition: to make a building or a place look good again by repairing or painting it

examples.x: The homeowners decided to renovate their kitchen, installing new cabinets and countertops.
She hired a contractor to renovate the outdated bathroom, replacing the fixtures and tiles.
The school board allocated funds to renovate the classrooms and improve the learning environment.
After years of neglect, the community rallied together to renovate the local park and playground.
The hotel management chose to renovate the lobby, giving it a modern and welcoming atmosphere.

senses.definition: a one-story construction without stairs, usually with a low roof

examples.x: The bungalow had a spacious backyard, perfect for hosting summer barbecues.
The couple decided to downsize and move into a cozy bungalow in the countryside.
The family rented a charming bungalow in the countryside for their summer vacation.
The bungalow featured a beautifully landscaped garden with a variety of tropical plants and flowers.

senses.definition: an area surrounded by walls or buildings, often part of a large house

examples.x: The grand manor boasted a beautiful inner court, adorned with lush greenery and elegant stone pathways, providing a serene retreat for its residents.
In the heart of the ancient castle, the inner court served as a gathering place for nobles and courtiers, its towering walls echoing with tales of bygone eras.
Behind the imposing façade of the mansion lay a secluded court, a private sanctuary where the elite could discuss matters of utmost importance away from prying eyes.
Within the confines of the stately villa, the central court exuded an atmosphere of refinement, its meticulously manicured gardens and ornate fountain adding to its allure.
The opulent palace featured an expansive inner court, complete with marble statues and intricately designed pavilions, offering a lavish setting for royal receptions and ceremonies.

senses.definition: a building's roof that is rounded

examples.x: The majestic cathedral was topped with a towering dome that gleamed in the sunlight.
The planetarium's dome provided a stunning backdrop for the immersive celestial show.
The mosque's central prayer hall was crowned by a magnificent dome adorned with intricate geometric patterns.
The library's reading room featured a stunning glass dome that flooded the space with natural light.
The museum's rotunda was capped with a soaring dome, creating an impressive architectural focal point.

senses.definition: a small room that is often heated with steam and has wooden walls, where people sit for relaxation or health benefits

examples.x: After a long day of skiing, they relaxed their muscles in the hotel's traditional Finnish sauna.
The spa's luxurious sauna was equipped with heated cedar benches and aromatic steam.
The health club's sauna provided a tranquil retreat for members to unwind and detoxify.
The wellness center offered a variety of treatments, including hot stone massages and infrared sauna sessions.
She enjoyed the calming sensation of sweating out toxins in the dry heat of the sauna.

senses.definition: a house or shelter in the shape of a dome that is built from blocks of ice or hard snow

examples.x: In the Arctic, indigenous people have been building traditional igloos for generations to withstand harsh winter conditions.
The Inuit family constructed a cozy igloo using blocks of ice to protect themselves from the freezing Arctic winds.
Children in northern regions enjoy building miniature igloos out of snow during the winter months.
During the blizzard, the stranded mountaineers took refuge in an improvised igloo made from compacted snow blocks to stay warm and safe.

senses.definition: a very tall building with many floors

examples.x: The city skyline was dotted with towering high-rises, each competing for prominence.
She worked on the top floor of a high-rise, enjoying panoramic views of the city below.
The neighborhood was undergoing rapid development, with new high-rises springing up every year.
Living in a high-rise can offer convenience and amenities, but some prefer the charm of low-rise buildings.
The architect's design for the new high-rise incorporated green spaces and sustainable features.

senses.definition: a large structure, such as a tower, placed near the coast and equipped with a powerful light that guides or warns the approaching ships

examples.x: The old lighthouse guided ships safely through the treacherous waters of the harbor.
Standing tall on the rocky coastline, the lighthouse served as a beacon of hope for sailors.
As dusk fell, the lighthouse began to glow, casting its light across the darkening ocean.
The lighthouse stood as a symbol of resilience, weathering countless storms and centuries of maritime history.

senses.definition: a long platform built from the shore into the sea that people can go for entertainment or a walk

examples.x: Families strolled along the pier, enjoying the salty breeze and panoramic views of the ocean.
At the end of the pier, fishermen cast their lines into the sparkling waters in hopes of catching dinner.
The pier was lined with colorful shops and eateries, enticing visitors with their seaside charm.
Children laughed and played at the water's edge, skipping stones and searching for seashells along the pier.
Local artists displayed their work along the pier, attracting admirers with their talent and creativity.

senses.definition: the building and its surrounding land owned or used by a business

examples.x: The company's premises included a modern office building surrounded by landscaped gardens.
Visitors were required to sign in at the reception desk before entering the premises.
The premises were equipped with state-of-the-art security systems to ensure the safety of employees and assets.
Signs were posted throughout the premises reminding employees to practice proper hygiene and social distancing.
The landlord conducted regular inspections to ensure that tenants were maintaining the premises in good condition.

senses.definition: a curved symmetrical structure that supports the weight above it, used in bridges or buildings

examples.x: The Golden Gate Bridge in San Francisco is a famous example of a suspension bridge with massive arches.
The architect designed a breathtaking building with soaring arches that created an open and airy interior space.
The ancient Roman aqueducts featured stone arches that allowed water to flow smoothly across long distances.
The castle's drawbridge was supported by a sturdy stone arch, which added to its defensive strength.
The cathedral's stained glass windows were framed by intricate stone arches, showcasing impressive Gothic architecture.

senses.definition: a long bar of iron or metal that supports the weight of a building

examples.x: The construction workers carefully lifted the steel beam into place, securing it to the framework of the building.
The ancient castle's stone beams had withstood centuries of wear and tear, a testament to their durability.
Engineers inspected the support beams of the bridge, ensuring they could withstand heavy loads and extreme weather conditions.
Sunlight streamed through the skylight, illuminating the wooden beams of the rustic farmhouse.
The architect designed the modern office space with exposed ceiling beams, giving it an industrial-chic aesthetic.

senses.definition: a gray powdery substance that becomes hard if it is mixed with water and sand, used for construction purposes such as sticking bricks of a wall together

examples.x: The construction crew poured cement into the foundation trenches, beginning the process of building the new house.
The sidewalks were cracked and uneven, in need of repair and fresh cement.
The ancient Roman aqueducts were constructed using a mixture of cement, sand, and volcanic ash.
She smoothed the wet cement with a trowel, carefully shaping it into the desired form for the garden path.

senses.definition: a flat piece of baked clay or other material, mostly in the shape of a square, used for covering floors or walls

examples.x: The kitchen floor was covered in ceramic tiles, creating a clean and durable surface that was easy to clean.
The bathroom walls were adorned with colorful mosaic tiles, adding a touch of elegance to the space.
The roof of the house was made of terracotta tiles, providing excellent insulation and weather protection.
The interior designer recommended subway tiles for the backsplash in the modern kitchen renovation.
The swimming pool was lined with mosaic tiles, creating a shimmering mosaic pattern on the water's surface.

senses.definition: a type of hard smooth rock that is mostly white in color and has colored lines, which is used as building material or in making statues

examples.x: The grand staircase in the mansion was adorned with marble steps, gleaming in the sunlight that streamed through the stained glass windows.
The sculptor carefully chiseled away at the block of marble, revealing the form of a graceful figure emerging from the stone.
The elegant ballroom featured marble columns and intricate marble flooring, creating a sense of opulence and luxury.
The museum displayed a collection of ancient Greek statues carved from white marble, depicting gods and goddesses in graceful poses.
The kitchen countertops were made of polished marble, adding a touch of sophistication to the modern design.

senses.definition: a very large tall machine used for lifting heavy objects

examples.x: The construction site buzzed with activity as the crane lifted steel beams into place for the new skyscraper.
A massive crane loomed over the shipyard, hoisting cargo containers onto waiting vessels.
The firefighters used a specialized crane to rescue the stranded cat from the top of the tree.
The port relied on cranes to unload cargo ships, transferring containers from the docks to waiting trucks.
The film crew set up a crane to capture sweeping aerial shots of the city skyline for the movie.

senses.definition: a staircase that moves and takes people up or down different levels easily, often found in large buildings like airports, department stores, etc.

examples.x: The weary travelers rode the escalator down to the baggage claim area after a long flight.
The department store installed a new escalator to improve accessibility between floors for shoppers.
She hurriedly descended the escalator, anxious to catch her train before it departed.
The airport terminal was bustling with passengers riding escalators to reach their departure gates.
He stood patiently on the escalator, enjoying the leisurely ascent to the top floor of the shopping mall.

senses.definition: a new room added to a building

examples.x: The architect drew up plans for an addition that would seamlessly blend with the original structure of the historic mansion.
The growing family decided to build an addition onto their house to accommodate their expanding needs.
She converted the garage into a cozy living space, creating an addition that served as a private guest suite.
The hospital announced plans for a new wing addition to provide state-of-the-art facilities for patient care.
The contractor estimated the cost of the home addition, factoring in materials, labor, and permits.

senses.definition: the materials that make up the floor of a building or room

examples.x: The interior designer selected eco-friendly bamboo flooring for the sustainable office renovation project.
He carefully measured the room before purchasing new laminate flooring for the kitchen remodel.
The construction crew laid down ceramic tile flooring in the bathroom, creating a sleek and modern look.

senses.definition: a hard layer of cement, stone, etc. that serves as the underground support of a building

examples.x: The construction crew poured concrete to create a solid foundation for the new office building.
The historic lighthouse stood firm atop its rocky foundation, weathering centuries of storms.
The engineer inspected the foundation of the bridge to ensure it could withstand heavy traffic.
The homeowner hired a contractor to repair the cracked foundation of their house, preventing further structural damage.
The architect designed the house with a raised foundation to mitigate the risk of flooding in the coastal area.

senses.definition: the specific way by which a building, book page, garden, etc. is arranged

examples.x: The architect presented several options for the layout of the new office space, each maximizing natural light and efficiency.
The magazine editor reworked the layout of the publication, adjusting the placement of articles and images for better flow.
She sketched out the layout of her garden, carefully planning the placement of flowers, shrubs, and pathways.
The graphic designer experimented with different layouts for the website, focusing on user experience and accessibility.
The interior decorator considered the layout of the furniture in the living room, aiming for both functionality and aesthetics.

senses.definition: a building used as the office or residence of the officials who represent their government in another country

examples.x: The ambassador hosted a reception at the embassy to welcome visiting dignitaries.
The American embassy in Paris is located near the Champs-Élysées.
She visited the embassy to obtain a visa for her upcoming trip abroad.
Security was heightened around the embassy following reports of a potential threat.
The embassy staff worked tirelessly to assist citizens stranded in the foreign country during the crisis.

senses.definition: the system of pipes for the distribution of water in a building

examples.x: The plumbing in the old house needed extensive repairs due to leaks and corrosion.
The plumber installed new fixtures as part of the plumbing renovation in the bathroom.
He learned the basics of plumbing to be able to handle minor repairs around the house.
The apartment complex experienced a plumbing emergency when a pipe burst in the basement.

senses.definition: a wooden house with a steep sloping roof, often found in mountainous areas in Switzerland

examples.x: The family rented a cozy chalet in the Swiss Alps for their winter vacation.
The chalet nestled among the pine trees offered stunning views of the snow-capped mountains.
She dreamt of owning a charming chalet overlooking a pristine alpine lake.
Guests gathered around the fireplace in the chalet's common area to unwind after a day of skiing.
The chalet's wooden beams and sloping roof added to its alpine charm.

senses.definition: a single piece of glass in a window

examples.x: The winter storm rattled the windowpanes, sending shivers down her spine.
He carefully cleaned each windowpane to ensure a crystal-clear view of the garden.
A tiny bird perched on the windowpane, chirping cheerfully as if seeking entry.
She traced her finger along the frost patterns forming on the windowpane.
The morning sunlight streamed through the windowpane, warming the room with its golden glow.

senses.definition: the area just inside the entrance of a public building such as a hotel, etc.

examples.x: Guests gathered in the hotel lobby, waiting for the shuttle to the airport.
The spacious lobby of the theater buzzed with excitement before the show.
She sat in the lobby, sipping her coffee and watching people come and go.
The lobby of the office building featured modern art installations and comfortable seating areas.
The hotel's grand lobby was adorned with marble floors and chandeliers.

senses.definition: (of a house or building) having a roof made of dried straw, leaves etc.

examples.x: The village was renowned for its picturesque thatched cottages, each with its own unique character.

senses.definition: (of a construction project) prepared for the building stage to begin

examples.x: The government's infrastructure plan aimed to create jobs by focusing on shovel-ready construction projects.
Investors were eager to fund shovel-ready projects that promised quick returns on investment.
The construction company prided itself on its ability to turn any site into a shovel-ready project in record time.
The mayor announced funding for several shovel-ready initiatives to revitalize the downtown area.
The real estate developer purchased the land with the intention of making it shovel-ready for residential construction.

senses.definition: to treat something valuable inconsiderately, in a way that affects it badly

examples.x: Many lanes are desecrated with yellow lines.
Ruinous new road schemes that desecrate the countryside.
The construction of an unsightly building desecrated the natural beauty of the landscape.
